calendar.matters:
SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Vernon L. Bradley, Esq. for Appellant/Defendant Alcombria L. Smith, also present.
Michael Blacksburg, Esq. for Respondent/Plaintiff Catherine Card, also present.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo, continued from January 14, 2010. The parties are sworn and the court hears argument regarding previously filed briefing. The following documentary evidence is introduced by Appellant/Defendant:
Exhs. 3.A & 3.B - Photos of tree before cutting
Exhs. 4.A & 4.B - Photos of tree before cutting
Exh. 5 - Photo
Exh. 6 - Photo of tree at fence line
Appellant's previously submitted exhibits will be included in the court's consideration.

The following documentary evidence is introduced as attachments to the trial brief filed this date by Respondent/Plaintiff:
Exh. C - Pltf's 2/25/09 Letter; Exh. D - Deft's 3/11/09 Letter; Exh. E - 3 Photos
Exh. F - Pltf's 3/19/09 Letter; Exh. G - Deft's 5/31/09 Letter with attached invoices;
Exh. H - 3 Photos; Exh. I - 10/1/09 ArborLogic Consulting Report; Exh. J - 9/29/09 Appraisal
Exh. K - Estimate of tree removal; Exh. L - 6 Photos, tree before and after

Matter taken under submission.

Judge Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: V. Mack, Not Reported (D318)

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-14-2010 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 318
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Vernon L. Bradley, Esq. for Appellant/Defendant/Cross Complainant Alcombria L. Smith, who is also present.
Catherine Card, in pro per Respondent/Plaintiff/Cross Defendant present.

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Catherine Card testifies on her own behalf. Under cross examination by Mr. Bradley, Esq., the following documentary evidence is introduced:
Exhibit 1 - Group of 14 photographs.
Exhibit 2 - CC&R's - Covenant, 41 pages.
Exhibit A - Group of photographs.
Exhibit B - 10/1/09 Letter to Catherine Card from James Lascot, 2 pages

The matter is continued to Feb. 23, 2010 at 9:00am in Dept.318 for the following 2 reasons: 1) Respondent/plaintiff's exhibits were not received in D318 at time of hearing, and for
2) Briefing on the issue of standing to sue raised by appellant's counsel re: is the tree located in the common area or owned by Ms. Card. (This issue was not raised as a basis for the appeal and was not included in the brief filed by Mr. Bradley, Esq.) Simultaneous briefing on this issue is due Feb. 8, 2010 and replies are due Feb. 16, 2010.

Judge Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: V. Mack, Not reported (D318)
